stemHtml:
A pilot is flying a single engine piston aircraft with a L/D ratio of 12.6:1, and is planning a flight over an area of dense forest with a length/width of 9.4 NM. The area around the forest is suitable for making a forced landing, in the event of an emergency. Calculate the minimum height above mean sea level at which the pilot can safely overfly the forest, given the following information:

Terrain elevation: 700 ft
Wind: Nil
1 NM = 6080 ft
